2603.16660.title: Can Linguistically Related Languages Guide LLM Translation in Low-Resource Settings?
2603.16660.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved strong performance across many downstream tasks, yet their effectiveness in extremely low-resource machine translation remains limited. Standard adaptation techniques typically rely on large-scale parallel data or extensive fine-tuning, which are infeasible for the long tail of underrepresented languages. In this work, we investigate a more constrained question: in data-scarce settings, to what extent can linguistically similar pivot languages and few-shot demonstrations provide useful guidance for on-the-fly adaptation in LLMs? We study a data-efficient experimental setup that combines linguistically related pivot languages with few-shot in-context examples, without any parameter updates, and evaluate translation behavior under controlled conditions. Our analysis shows that while pivot-based prompting can yield improvements in certain configurations, particularly in settings where the target language is less well represented in the model's vocabulary, the gains are often modest and sensitive to few shot example construction. For closely related or better represented varieties, we observe diminishing or inconsistent gains. Our findings provide empirical guidance on how and when inference-time prompting and pivot-based examples can be used as a lightweight alternative to fine-tuning in low-resource translation settings.
2603.16660.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在許多下游任務中表現出色，但在極低資源的機器翻譯中，其有效性仍然有限。標準的適應技術通常依賴於大規模的平行數據或廣泛的微調，這對於長尾的代表性不足語言來說是不可行的。在這項工作中，我們探討了一個更受限的問題：在數據稀缺的環境中，語言上相似的樞紐語言和少量示範在即時適應LLMs時能提供多大的有用指導？我們研究了一種數據高效的實驗設置，該設置結合了語言相關的樞紐語言和少量上下文示例，且不進行任何參數更新，並在受控條件下評估翻譯行為。我們的分析顯示，雖然基於樞紐的提示在某些配置中可以產生改進，特別是在目標語言在模型詞彙中代表性較低的情況下，但這些增益通常是適度的，並且對少量示例的構建敏感。對於密切相關或代表性較強的變體，我們觀察到增益減少或不一致。我們的發現為如何以及何時在低資源翻譯環境中使用推理時的提示和基於樞紐的示例作為輕量級替代微調提供了實證指導。
2603.16660.author: Aishwarya Ramasethu et.al.
2603.16660.authors: Aishwarya Ramasethu, Niyathi Allu, Rohin Garg, Harshwardhan Fartale, Dun Li Chan
